old_claim.note: Fire Control is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because the corrected horticulture scope is small-scale trial cultivation at Ohalo II, and the source-backed mechanism is wild-cereal gathering, harvesting, and cultivation rather than controlled fire.

source_shape.source_locator: The PLOS ONE paper supports Ohalo II small-scale trial cultivation through proto-weeds, wild cereals, domestic-type rachis scars, and sickle blades, without identifying fire control as a cultivation prerequisite.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source grounds the horticulture milestone in archaeobotanical and harvesting evidence at Ohalo II rather than in fire technology.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Fire control remains a much earlier general human technology, but attaching it directly to this cultivation node preserved an unrelated Wonderwerk fire citation instead of the plant-cultivation evidence now used for the corrected scope.

ontology_before: Horticulture directly depended on fire_control and cited a Wonderwerk Cave fire paper, implying controlled fire was a source-backed predecessor for early plant cultivation.
ontology_after: Horticulture is scoped to Ohalo II small-scale trial cultivation and depends directly on advanced hunter-gatherer context and plant foraging/botanical knowledge, with no direct fire-control edge.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: horticulture no longer directly depends on fire_control.
would_reject_if: The node were later rescoped to cooking, controlled burning, slash-and-burn cultivation, or fire-managed landscapes.
A source showed Ohalo II trial cultivation required controlled burning as part of the cultivation method.
The fire_control node were narrowed to include plant cultivation or field management rather than controlled combustion generally.
short_reason: The source-backed Ohalo II cultivation claim is botanical and harvesting evidence, not controlled fire.
